<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

This PR modernizes the email infrastructure by replacing Nodemailer/MailDev with Resend, removes the associated MailDev devtools panel, refactors build tooling, and standardizes CI/linting rules. The email migration updates environment validation, adds a new Resend adapter with error handling, includes comprehensive tests, wires the new adapter into authentication, and removes MailDev services and documentation. The devtools cleanup removes query/config infrastructure and the UI panel. Developer tooling improvements extract shared Git utilities into a central module, update build scripts to use these utilities with flexible file inputs, and establish consistent linting/formatting standards via updated CI workflows, local hooks, and Semgrep rules. Dependencies are upgraded throughout the stack.

## Possibly related PRs

- [hbmartin/start-ui-web#13](https://github.com/hbmartin/start-ui-web/pull/13): Changes email module typing while this PR removes Nodemailer entirely and replaces it with Resend adapter—direct overlap on email-sending implementation.
- [hbmartin/start-ui-web#8](https://github.com/hbmartin/start-ui-web/pull/8): Updates devtools MailDev integration via `configQueries.devtools()` while this PR removes that entire devtools query layer and panel—direct collision in query/config wiring.
- [hbmartin/start-ui-web#12](https://github.com/hbmartin/start-ui-web/pull/12): Also touches email adapter infrastructure, reshaping Nodemailer surface while this PR migrates away from it to Resend—code-level overlap in the email module area.

</details>

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

## Code Review

This pull request transitions the project's email infrastructure from Maildev and Nodemailer to Resend, which includes updating environment variables, removing the Maildev Docker service, and deleting the associated devtool panel. It also features a significant update to project dependencies, a refactor of git-related scripts into a shared utility, and the addition of new Semgrep rules for CI consistency. Review feedback highlights an unused import in the refactored scripts and suggests using a standard 500 status code for email delivery errors to prevent client-side misinterpretation of provider-specific status codes.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `spawnSync` import is no longer used in this file after refactoring the git logic into `scripts/lib/git-utils.mjs`. It should be removed to keep the code clean.

```suggestion
import { listChangedFiles, resolveBase } from './lib/git-utils.mjs';

```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Propagating the provider's status code directly (like 401 or 403) can be problematic. If Resend returns a 401 due to an invalid API key, and this error bubbles up to an API response, the client might misinterpret it as the user's own session being expired, potentially triggering an unwanted logout. Since this is a `system` category error, it's safer to return a generic 500 status to the client while keeping the original provider status in the `details` object for observability.

```suggestion
      status: 500,
```

Inline comments: In @.semgrep.yml: - Around line 186-194: The rule lefthook-format-changed-stage-fixed's pattern-regex is too narrow because it only skips when stage_fixed: true immediately follows run: pnpm format:changed; update the pattern-regex to ensure the negative check for stage_fixed:\s*true covers the entire format:changed block (not just the next line) by using a single-line/tempered-dot lookahead that scans the block for stage_fixed anywhere (e.g., use (?s) and a (?!.*stage_fixed:\s*true) style assertion scoped to the format block) so the rule is order-insensitive while still matching missing stage_fixed cases; modify the pattern-regex field in the lefthook-format-changed-stage-fixed rule accordingly. --- Outside diff comments: In `@README.md`: - Around line 52-53: Update the README line for the Docker init command to remove the stale "Maildev" mention: change the comment for pnpm dk:init so it only lists the services actually started (e.g., "Start Docker containers (PostgreSQL, MinIO)") and ensure any other references to Maildev in that section are deleted or adjusted to reflect the current stack; look for the exact string "pnpm dk:init" in the README to locate and edit the text. ```

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
python - <<'PY'
import re, textwrap

pattern = re.compile(r'(?s)pre-commit:.*?format:\s*\n(?=\s*run:\s*pnpm\s+format:changed[^\n]*\n(?!\s*stage_fixed:\s*true))')

cases = {
    "valid_immediate_stage_fixed": """
pre-commit:
  commands:
    format:
      run: pnpm format:changed -- {staged_files}
      stage_fixed: true
      glob: '*.{js,ts}'
""",
    "valid_stage_fixed_after_glob": """
pre-commit:
  commands:
    format:
      run: pnpm format:changed -- {staged_files}
      glob: '*.{js,ts}'
      stage_fixed: true
""",
    "invalid_missing_stage_fixed": """
pre-commit:
  commands:
    format:
      run: pnpm format:changed -- {staged_files}
      glob: '*.{js,ts}'
"""
}

for name, cfg in cases.items():
    matched = bool(pattern.search(textwrap.dedent(cfg)))
    print(f"{name}: matched={matched}")

print("\nExpected behavior:")
print("- valid_immediate_stage_fixed: matched=False")
print("- valid_stage_fixed_after_glob: matched=False")
print("- invalid_missing_stage_fixed: matched=True")
PY
```

Repository: hbmartin/start-ui-web

Length of output: 348

---



</details>

**Make `lefthook-format-changed-stage-fixed` order-insensitive for `stage_fixed: true` within the `format:changed` block.**
- In `.semgrep.yml` rule `lefthook-format-changed-stage-fixed`, the current `pattern-regex` only avoids triggering when `stage_fixed: true` is immediately after `run: pnpm format:changed`; when `stage_fixed: true` appears later in the same `format` block (e.g., after `glob`), the rule still matches and creates false positives.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In @.semgrep.yml around lines 186 - 194, The rule
lefthook-format-changed-stage-fixed's pattern-regex is too narrow because it
only skips when stage_fixed: true immediately follows run: pnpm format:changed;
update the pattern-regex to ensure the negative check for stage_fixed:\s*true
covers the entire format:changed block (not just the next line) by using a
single-line/tempered-dot lookahead that scans the block for stage_fixed anywhere
(e.g., use (?s) and a (?!.*stage_fixed:\s*true) style assertion scoped to the
format block) so the rule is order-insensitive while still matching missing
stage_fixed cases; modify the pattern-regex field in the
lefthook-format-changed-stage-fixed rule accordingly.
```

</details>
